observations.recommendation:
Management prioritize the compliance with COA Circular 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts with the end in view of reconciling the PPE balances in the FS against the Inventory Report; and 

Inventory Committee should complete the physical inventory in three months or less and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities, and ensure its full implementation.

Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant a copy of a detailed breakdown of remittances of withheld contributions as well as the incurred penalties and surcharges for CY 2023 and for the other years thereon, if any for proper reconciliation with the amounts withheld and remitted in the books to be able to prospectively remit the correct amount and prevent over-remittances.

Municipal Accountant reclassify the amount to its appropriate receivable account after validation of the amount of alleged penalties and surcharges included in the remittance of CY 2023 and prior years.

Management ensure that the: 

Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, verify the contributions withheld and loan amortizations deducted from the payroll in the previous years and in CY 2023 to determine its validity;




Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant a copy of a detailed breakdown of the remittances of withheld contributions and loan amortizations in CY 2023 and other years thereon as attachment in the disbursement voucher; and 

Accounting Office thoroughly review the deductions of personnel pertaining to GSIS premiums/loan amortization and ensure that these are fully remitted to the GSIS within the prescribed period.

Management should: 

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s Index Card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; 

Furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the certified list to serve as basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to record the receivables based on the list furnished by the Municipal Treasurer in order to recognize in the books an accurate and verifiable data pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1.

Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the request for write-off of the dormant receivables from COA following the procedures and requirements provided under COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Management confirm with the NEA of the status of the loans payable and thereafter carry out the appropriate action and prepare the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct amount of the loans payable account.

Management should:

Coordinate with the source agencies and request for authority to transfer the unexpended balances to the General Fund of the Municipality. Otherwise, return the unexpended balance to the source agencies, whichever is appropriate and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding liquidation report and other pertinent documents for post-audit; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to review the SLs with negative balances and trace the cause/s thereof and thereafter, make the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balances.  Also, indicate the appropriate descriptions in the subsidiary ledger such as the project/program names, source agencies and the date of the fund transfer to facilitate monitoring.

Management, through the Special Disbursing Officer, to maintain a CDR and record therein the receipts and disbursements daily as required in Section 7.0 of COA-DBM-DILG-GC for GOCCs-DND Joint Circular. Henceforth, strict compliance of the said Joint Circular is hereby enjoined.
Municipal Mayor exhaust all possible remedies to collect and to demand liquidation of fund transfer to NGO/PO, otherwise, request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.
Municipal Mayor exhaust all possible remedies to collect and to demand liquidation of cash advances, otherwise, request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.
Management direct the Municipal Accountant to request for the write-off of the dormant receivables from COA supported with documents required under COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
instruct the GSO to expedite the completion of the physical inventory taking and reconcile with the accounting records, and submit thereafter the copy of the report to the Audit Team for evaluation;
cause the reconstitution of the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of Municipality’s properties and prepare the RPCPPE to prevent recurrence of deficiency; and
require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to designate a staff in each office to prepare and maintain the required PPELCs and PCs for each category of PPE.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor make representation with NEA for a condonation of the interest and surcharges and make immediate payments to settle the account.
Also, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to coordinate with NEA for the correct amount of the principal, interest, and surcharges as basis for the recoding of the appropriate adjusting entries in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, who agreed to conduct thorough analysis and review of the accounts and subsequently prepare adjusting entry to correct the balances of the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, who agreed to trace the supporting documents of each payable account to ascertain the existence of actual claims, and settle immediately if found to be valid, otherwise make necessary reversal of the account to present the correct balances of these liability accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, who agreed to send demand letters to the debtors and assess the validity and collectability of each account for appropriate remedial action including the initiation of the procedures under COA Circular 2023-008 for the write-off of the accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor through the concerned officials, agreed to exert more effort to locate the MOA and other related documents of the transactions and send formal letters to the NGOs/POs requesting the submission of the liquidation reports for funds transferred. Otherwise, submit request for authority to write-off from COA for the fund transferred to the dissolved NGO/PO, if all means of collection already exhausted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator as well as other concerned Offices, who agreed to coordinate and collectively formulate a comprehensive and realistic plan to expedite the timely procurement and implementation of all identified priority development projects under the 20 per cent DF and ensure that programs and projects incorporated in the Plan are all “Implementation Ready” to guarantee the prompt accomplishment of the same.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and other concerned municipal officials and employees to reconcile their respective records and conduct regular monitoring to ensure that fund transfers are implemented and liquidated within the prescribed period;
Concerned officials to return or seek authority from the source agencies to realign the other excess/unspent balances of the fully implemented projects. Likewise, assess the feasibility/necessity of the projects that remain unimplemented, otherwise return the funds to the source agency; and
Municipal accountant to conduct analysis of the accounts with negative balances and identify the causes thereon and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal GAD Focal Person and other concerned department heads, who agreed to coordinate and ensure that all identified GAD PPAs in their respective offices included in the GAD Plan and Budget be implemented.

We recommended and Management agreed through the Municipal Accountant and the Payroll-in-Charge to make representation with these government agencies for the reconciliation of the accounts and consequently take appropriate action as needed.

GSO/concerned offices conduct the physical inventory of supplies and materials in accordance with existing rules and regulations to verify the accuracy of reported balances and submit the required report thereon to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and 

GSO and Accounting personnel review the transactions, reconcile and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct account balances.

Municipal Accountant investigate the difference of both records and submit a reconciliation statement between the reported balance by the Municipal Treasurer and that of the accounting records of Cash - Local Treasury account; and

Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile their records to timely detect errors in recordings and effect the necessary corrections.

Municipal Accountant record the actual transfer of the remaining unexpended balance to the TF in the amount of ₱2,826,002.46 for CY 2023 and ₱156,000.00 for CY 2022 with the following proposed adjusting journal entries:

In General Fund Books

Dr. Prior Period Adjustment ₱2,982,002.46 
Cr. Cash in Bank- LCCA ₱2,982,002.46 
To record the transfer of unspent LDRRM funds

 In Trust Fund Books

Dr. Cash in Local Treasury ₱2,982,002.46
Cr. Trust Liability –DRRM ₱2,982,002.46
To record the receipt of unspent LDRRM funds from General Fund

Municipal Engineer submit a written justification/ explanation on the:


Granting of contract time extensions due to price escalation when the same is not one of the grounds stipulated in Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and

Granting of contract time extensions which exceeded the original contract time contrary to the limitations set by the Procurement Law and its IRR as well as the Manual of Procedures for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects for local government units.

The Chairperson of Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accountant should consider requiring the contractors of the two completed concreting projects to extend the validity of the respective performance securities up to one year from completion date or to cover the defects liability period, if feasible, or require additional/new performance securities to guarantee that the contractors perform their responsibilities stated in Section 62.2.2.1 of the IRR of RA 9184.

Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant take definite action on the following:

Regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with the accounting records in order to timely effect the necessary corrections and consistently harmonize the balances of both records; and


Adjust cashbook records of the CIB- LCCA in the GF, SEF and TF as provided in the Bank Reconciliation Statement as at December 31, 2023 in order to establish the correct accountability.
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer submit the revised LDRRMFIP pursuant to the requirements provided in the JMC.

Municipal Treasurer immediately remit all taxes withheld to avoid further penalties; 



Municipal Accountant regularly check the amount of withholding taxes remitted and ensure that it reconciles with the amount of the taxes withheld for the same period; and

Municipal Accountant cause the verification, tracing and validation of the beginning balances and prepare a schedule showing the name of creditors and the corresponding unremitted amount of taxes withheld to support the Due to BIR account balance.
DRRM Officer submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and henceforth, observe the timely submission thereof.

Management direct the LDRRMO on the strict compliance with the submission of monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the COA Auditor to properly monitor the projects and activities and their implementation.

Municipal Engineer observe the duration of project implementation as per Program of Work to prevent the incurrence of any negative slip/ additional cost and such that intended beneficiaries could avail of the facilities/services.

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the request for write-off of the dormant receivables from COA following the procedures and requirements provided under COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

The Management to confirm with the NEA of the status of the loans payable and thereafter carry out the appropriate action and prepare the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct amount of the loans payable account.

The Management, through the Special Disbursing Officer, to maintain a CDR and record therein the receipts and disbursements daily as required in Section 7.0 of COA-DBM-DILG-GC for GOCCs-DND Joint Circular. Henceforth, strict compliance of the said Joint Circular is hereby enjoined.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exhaust all possible remedies to collect and to demand liquidation of cash advances, otherwise, request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.

The Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to request for the write-off of the dormant receivables from COA supported with documents required under COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

The Management to direct the LDRRMO on the strict compliance with the submission of monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the COA Auditor to properly monitor the projects and activities and their implementation.

We recommended and the Municipal Finance Committee agreed to submit:
A supplemental budget with concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan containing the deficient allocation in the amount of ₱45,228.85 for LDRRMF to effect the changes in the budget; and
A revised LDRRMFIP to include the ₱185,000.00 and the deficient amount of ₱45,228.85 or a total of ₱230,228.85 to be allocated as additional 70 per cent preparedness and mitigation fund with corresponding specific PPAs and 30 per cent quick response fund shown in lump sum.

The RHU to furnish a copy of the Inventory of COVID-19 vaccine report to the Accounting Office for the latter to further verify, and thereafter take-up the verified balance in the Municipality’s books of accounts to adjust balances of both the inventory and donation accounts and henceforth, both offices should observe proper coordination in order to prevent similar omissions/ errors in their future operations considering that the vaccination is still a continued priority program in 2023.

The RHU shall properly monitor all inventories and donations received and transferred by the Municipality and ensure that these are all documented.

The Management to direct the Municipality’s Planning and Development Office and the Municipality’s Local Development Council to include in the AIP and eventually in the proposed budget only those programs and projects that are “Implementation Ready”, which includes those projects with thorough study/investigation, plans, program of works and estimates prior to their inclusion thereof to avoid delays and ensure the immediate implementation of the projects.

The Management to evaluate the viability of the projects that were not implemented during the year funded from current and continuing appropriations for appropriate realignment.
We recommended that Management submit a valid written justification on the deficiencies noted for the audit team’s validation to prevent the issuance of Notice of Suspension on the transaction.

The Management to submit an amended LDRRMFIP that includes the list of projects and activities charged against the unexpended LDRRMF in the previous years in conformity with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and

Submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and henceforth, observe the timely submission thereof."

The Municipal Budget Officer to properly classify the PPAs for LDRRMF as MOOE or CO and maintain a RAAO for the continuing appropriation of the LDRRMF for CO.

The Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer to determine the balances of CO in the prior years’ transfer of LDRRMF and make the necessary adjusting journal entries. The Management to instruct the Municipal Assessor to ensure that the Assessment Roll and other records maintained by their Office are accurate and updated as this shall be furnished to the Local Treasurer as basis for the collection of real property taxes and for updating the RPTAR/ RPTIC. We also recommend that the properties are properly classified as whether taxable or exempt from tax pursuant to the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations.

The Management, through the DO-Designate, to review and verify the payroll attachments and recompute, if needed, in order to get the exact net payroll to be drawn for cash advances and in as much as some of the JOs and personnel are from the mountain barangays, the DO shall inform them through a notice that cash for the payment of payrolls will be returned to the depository bank of the Municipality if unclaimed within five days after each 15th day/end of the month pay period.
The Management to direct the DO-Designate to update the cashbook and ensure to record the refunds made in the cashbook as part of the liquidation of the CAs pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

The SDO shall return or refund immediately to the Municipal Treasurer any excess CA for special project activities which is no longer needed or has not been used for a period of two months and strictly observe the rules and procedures on the proper liquidation of CA pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 to avoid the recurrence of this deficiency.
The Management to discontinue granting cash advances in excess of the maximum cash accountability of the SDO.

The Management to submit a valid written justification on the noted deficiencies for evaluation by the audit team. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the guidelines on the hiring of contract of service pursuant to COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2 s.2020 and RA No. 9184.

The Management to submit a valid written justification on the incurrence of the above-cited expenses which are considered unnecessary subject to the Audit Team’s evaluation. Henceforth, Management should practice prudence in the disbursement of government funds.

The Management to submit a valid written justification on the grant of transportation allowance to the herein officer who was already assigned a government vehicle and henceforth, discontinue the granting thereof pursuant to the above-mentioned circular and SC ruling.

Require the Municipal Accountant to trace the entries involving the reciprocal accounts and to examine if it is necessary to remain as an Intra-Agency Receivable or as an Intra-Agency Payable; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments if needed to be able to reflect a correct and accurate report and that all reciprocal accounts should reflect zero balances as at a given period.

     Due from NGOs/Pos  ₱125,000.00
         Due from GOCCs        ₱125,000.00

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Property Officer to conduct complete physical count of PPE as of December 31, 2021, render the report thereon and provide a copy of the report to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124, Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and/or Property and Supplier Officer maintain property cards for monitoring and reconciliation with the Property Ledger Card of the Accounting Office; and
We further recommended that Management through the Inventory Committee carry out the procedures prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 in order to present the correct balances of the PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
require the Municipal Accountant to trace the entries involving the reciprocal accounts and to examine if it is necessary to remain as an Intra-Agency Receivable or as an Intra-Agency Payable; and
require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments if needed to be able to reflect a correct and accurate report and that all reciprocal accounts should reflect zero balances as at a given period.

We recommended and  the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to request for the write-off of the dormant receivables from COA supported with documents required under COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the subsidiary account balance of said fund transfer to the Due from NGOs/POs account by taking up the following adjusting journal entry:
Due from NGOs/POs		₱125,000.00
Due from GOCCs			₱125,000.00
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor exhaust all possible remedies to collect and to demand liquidation of fund transfer to NGO/PO, otherwise, request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the projects and undertake actions when contractors incur negative slippage in the implementation of infrastructure projects pursuant to the calibrated measures provided under GPPB Circular No. 03-2019; and impose liquidated damages to the erring contractor pursuant to provisions of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management impose liquidated damages for late deliveries pursuant to Section 68 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and its related issuances to ensure that suppliers/contractors engaging business with the government comply with the conditions of the contract and avoid any delay in the delivery of the procured goods/items.
We recommended that Management submit valid explanation and the documents mentioned as lacking to ascertain validity and propriety of the contract/transaction by the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to release the RPT share to Barangays, on a quarterly basis, in order for the latter to utilize the funds for its priority projects.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the BAC Secretariat to follow the guide on how to create bid and award notice in the posting portal of the PHILGEPs website. We further recommend that the BAC Secretariat comply with the requirements of Section 37.1.6 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 on the posting of the Notices of Award and to ensure that all the details are correct so that the intended transparency of the procurement process of the agency can be achieved.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exhaust all possible remedies to collect and to demand liquidation of cash advances, otherwise, request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require all the depository banks to strictly comply with the submission of the mandated BS and supporting documents within five days after the end of each month. In turn, within ten days from receipt of the said BS, the same should be reconciled with the GL which shall be the basis in the preparation of the BRS. Finally, the BRS must be regularly submitted to the Audit Team within ten days every after the end of each month together with the cancelled and paid checks and debit/credit memos.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Quarterly Trial Balance and all schedules supporting the account balances within the reglementary period which is not later than the 20th day of the following month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to generate the RAO and the SAAOB for CY2021 and submit the same to the Audit Team through the Municipal Accountant. Henceforth, strict compliance with the above-stated provisions is hereby enjoined.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the LDRRM Officer to submit the supplemental LDRRMFIP to the LDC for inclusion of the PAPs to the AIP in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC NO. 2013-1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the LDRRMO to ensure strict compliance with the submission of monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF to the COA Auditor to properly monitor the projects and activities and their implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the erroneous recording of completed Infra-Asset to its appropriate PPE account so as not to compromise the fair and consistent presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that an inventory committee be created to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the properties of the municipality every year, and submit a properly filled-up and signed inventory report to the local chief executive, a copy of which is to be furnished to the office of the auditor. Reconciliation with accounting records shall then be made and the municipal accountant shall then prepare adjusting entries, if necessary, to come up with a reliable PPE account balances in the financial statements. Also, property records such as property cards and ledger cards must always be maintained by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant, respectively, to facilitate reconciliation with the physical inventory and immediate verification of discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: The designated General Services Officer (GSO) informed that he has already prepared and updated the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
observations.reason: The GSO has prepared the RPCPPE duly signed by the Inventory Committee which was created by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned Municipal, Philippine National Police, Department of Interior and Local Government and Commission On Election personnel refund the amount of P145,932.90, otherwise, the officials concerned who approved the payment thereof should be held liable for the payment thereof, notwithstanding the filing of appropriate charges against them.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer has to secure first the copy of the payroll for overtime of personnel who served during May 2016 election and to instruct the personnel concerned who is still connected in the service to refund the amount.
observations.reason: The Municipality has secured a copy of the letter from the COMELEC authorizing the said expenses, however, the former was willing to refund the overtime pay as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the amount of P258,701.46 immediately and all her collections intact to the depository bank pursuant to Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992. Corollary thereto, in view of the distance of the Municipality of Boljoon to the depository bank in Cebu City, we recommend that Management transfer its depository bank, through a Sangguniang Bayan (SB) resolution, to Land Bank of the Philippines – Dalaguete Branch, so that the Municipal Treasurer could deposit her collections immediately and conveniently. On the other hand, require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a Report of Collections based on her cashbook so that the amount of P30,046.91 could be taken up in the books.
observations.managementAction: Last August 1, 2017 the Municipality of Boljoon had just transferred its old depository bank to LBP- Dalaguete. As a result, undeposited collections were minimized.
observations.reason: The Municipality had already opened a checking account in LBP Dalaguete resulting in the minimal amount of undeposited collections at month-end. Collections could not be fully deposited as there was no cut-off established by Management. Accordingly, they were advised to establish a cut-off time, if possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all concerned officials and employees be directed to refund the P6,000.00 excess amount of PEI received otherwise, all those involved who authorized the payment thereof shall be held personally liable therefore.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that the employees have been deducted of their refunds thru payroll and that the remaining balance have been minimal.
observations.reason: The post-audit of paid payrolls showed that the Management personnel have been deducted of their disallowances thru payroll deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accountant should maintain separate, accurate subsidiary records for each trust liability regardless of whether a separate bank account is maintained for each fund or not pursuant to Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 94-013. Likewise, she must return to the Source Agency any unused balance upon completion of the project and to observe the limitation on the use of the Fund Transfer pursuant to Section 9.0 of the same Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant justified that the MOAs for the previous years could not be located. Currently, MOAs have been asked for receipt of funds from source agencies. The balances represent funds for unimplemented projects. For those which have been implemented, the excess will be returned to the Treasury.
observations.reason: Verification disclosed that MOAs, Sub-allotment Advice or SARO have been attached to funds received by the Municipality. However, excess funds or balances for the implemented projects has yet to be returned to the Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Engineer as well as the GSO inform COA in writing on the facts surrounding the loss/disappearance of the various INFRA Projects costing P597,082.15 including the DSWD office. Likewise, explain in writing the whereabouts of the serviceable Motor Vehicle valued at P299,850.00 and other construction materials including the two (2) units shredder machines, otherwise, the concerned personnel may be held liable for the cost therefor. Require an explanation by the Municipal Engineer on why a Temporary Structure was built in view of the location of the building and on why the project was not continued considering that funds were available for the construction of a permanent structure which resulted in wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and Treasurer as well as the concerned Department of Social Welfare and Development informed that they have already informed COA on the loss thereof.
observations.reason: The concerned personnel were advised to prepare and submit Request for Relief to COA and not just a letter informing COA of the loss such that they have a chance to be relieved for their accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management intensify its tax collection drive by issuing regular billings or notices and making personal follow-ups on delinquent taxpayers.  The LGU should avail of all legal remedies either by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Sections 256, 257 and 258 of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer informed that she has sent collection letters to these delinquent taxpayers. The assistant Municipal Treasurer also narrated that although not all have paid their taxes, at least they already knew who are the persons having delinquent accounts.
observations.reason: Verification disclosed that RPT Receivables have decreased during the year.

observations.recommendation: We require management to submit a written explanation/ justification as to why the Toyota Fortuner was simultaneously withdrawing both diesel and gasoline and why it was gassing up more than its maximum fuel capacity, before the said expenses are disallowed in audit. We further recommend that management should strictly enforce Commission on Audit Circular No. 77-61, more particularly, the implementation of the following: a. Require the drivers to properly accomplish the Driver’s Trip Ticket (DTT) and to fill up all the required data. b. Require the drivers to prepare a Monthly Report of Official Travels for each vehicle at the end of each month. This should summarize in chronological order his trips for the month. The report shall be certified by the driver and approved by the Administrative Officer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that she has sent collection letter to the former Municipal Mayor and to deduct from her terminal leave benefits once she filed for claims of her benefits.
observations.reason: The said practice had been stopped by Management as that was incurred only by the previous Mayor. This Office has yet to receive a copy of the said Collection Letter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Bids and Awards  Committee  (BAC)  and
    the Municipal officials concerned compute  for  the  liquidated  damages
    representing delay in the deliveries of the 477  bags  of  rice  and  to
    fully account for the lacking 406  bags  or  20,306  kilos  of  rice  to
    prevent disallowances in audit.

       Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the supplier to submit an
    Official or Collection Receipt to  support  payment  received  from  the
    Municipality. In addition, explain why a direct purchase  was  not  made
    from the National Food Authority who is a government  agency  authorized
    to sell rice at a minimal price such that the beneficiaries  could  have
    received more than what they deserved.

      Henceforth, the BAC  and  the  concerned  Municipal  Officials  should
    ensure that public funds be spent wisely by  procuring  items  available
    from authorized government dealer and to see to it that procurements are
    fully accounted for. Any delay in  the  deliveries  thereof,  should  be
    charged liquidated damages.

We recommend that the concerned Municipal, Philippine National Police,
    Department of Interior and Local Government and Commission  On  Election
    personnel refund the amount of  P145,932.90,  otherwise,  the  officials
    concerned who approved the payment thereof should be held liable for the
    payment thereof,  notwithstanding  the  filing  of  appropriate  charges
    against them.

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Disaster  Risk-Reduction  Management
    (MDRRM) Officer and Management officials concerned review the MDRRM  Plan
    on whether the projects and programs to  be  undertaken  is  directly  in
    connection with the prevention and relief of calamities.

      Henceforth, stop the charging of expenditures  under  the  LDRRM  Fund
    which is not in pursuance of the above-mentioned provisions.

We recommend that the  Municipal  Treasurer  deposit  the  amount  of
    P258,701.46 immediately and all her collections intact to the depository
    bank pursuant to Section 28 of COA Circular No.  92-382  dated  July  3,
    1992. Corollary thereto, in view of the distance of the Municipality  of
    Boljoon  to  the  depository  bank  in  Cebu  City,  we  recommend  that
    Management transfer its depository bank,  through  a  Sangguniang  Bayan
    (SB) resolution, to Land Bank of the Philippines – Dalaguete Branch,  so
    that the Municipal Treasurer could deposit her  collections  immediately
    and conveniently.


       On the other hand, require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  prepare  a
    Report of Collections based on  her  cashbook  so  that  the  amount  of
    P30,046.91 could be taken up in the books.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the above-cited
    transactions to its proper Property,  Plant  and  Equipment  account  by
    drawing a Journal  Entry  Voucher  to  correct  the  entries  that  were
    erroneously made.

            Henceforth, strict  compliance  with  the  above  provisions  is
    hereby    enjoined.
